findings.title: Command injection via MANUALINPUT: Command substitution $(pwd)
findings.details:
Script: SecTest-UnanchoredRegex
Validator: [a-zA-Z0-9]+ (type=0)
Payload: 'abc$(pwd)'
Output: OUTPUT_abc/var/lib/zabbix_END


Root cause: substitute_macro() in nodecommand.c performs raw string replacement of {MANUALINPUT} into shell commands without any escaping. The regex validation uses zbx_regexp_match_full() which performs PCRE partial matching (not anchored), so an unanchored regex like [a-zA-Z0-9]+ passes 'abc$(pwd)' because 'abc' satisfies the partial match. The command is then executed via execl('/bin/sh', 'sh', '-c', command).
findings.remediation:
1. Shell-escape the MANUALINPUT value before substitution into commands (e.g., replace single quotes and wrap in single quotes).
2. Enforce anchored regex matching: automatically wrap validator regex in ^...$ if not already anchored, or use pcre2_match with PCRE2_ANCHORED.
3. Use argument-based execution instead of shell string interpolation: pass MANUALINPUT as an environment variable rather than substituting into the command string.
4. Add a character allowlist/blocklist for MANUALINPUT values to reject shell metacharacters ($, `, \, etc.).

findings.title: Command injection via MANUALINPUT: Command substitution $(id)
findings.details:
Script: SecTest-UnanchoredRegex
Validator: [a-zA-Z0-9]+ (type=0)
Payload: 'abc$(id)'
Output: OUTPUT_abcuid=1997(zabbix) gid=1995(zabbix) groups=1995(zabbix),20(dialout)_END

The regex validation uses zbx_regexp_match_full() which performs PCRE partial matching (not anchored), so an unanchored regex like [a-zA-Z0-9]+ passes 'abc$(pwd)' because 'abc' satisfies the partial match. findings.title: Command injection via MANUALINPUT: Backtick command substitution
findings.details:
Script: SecTest-UnanchoredRegex
Validator: [a-zA-Z0-9]+ (type=0)
Payload: 'abc`pwd`'
Output: OUTPUT_abc/var/lib/zabbix_END


Root cause: substitute_macro() in nodecommand.c performs raw string replacement of {MANUALINPUT} into shell commands without any escaping. The regex validation uses zbx_regexp_match_full() which performs PCRE partial matching (not anchored), so an unanchored regex like [a-zA-Z0-9]+ passes 'abc$(pwd)' because 'abc' satisfies the partial match. The command is then executed via execl('/bin/sh', 'sh', '-c', command).

2. Enforce anchored regex matching: automatically wrap validator regex in ^...$ if not already anchored, or use pcre2_match with PCRE2_ANCHORED.

4. Add a character allowlist/blocklist for MANUALINPUT values to reject shell metacharacters ($, `, \, etc.).

findings.title: Command injection via MANUALINPUT: Variable expansion ${PATH}
findings.details:
Script: SecTest-UnanchoredRegex
Validator: [a-zA-Z0-9]+ (type=0)
Payload: 'abc${PATH}'
Output: OUTPUT_abc/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin_END


Root cause: substitute_macro() in nodecommand.c performs raw string replacement of {MANUALINPUT} into shell commands without any escaping. The regex validation uses zbx_regexp_match_full() which performs PCRE partial matching (not anchored), so an unanchored regex like [a-zA-Z0-9]+ passes 'abc$(pwd)' because 'abc' satisfies the partial match. The command is then executed via execl('/bin/sh', 'sh', '-c', command).
findings.remediation:
1. Shell-escape the MANUALINPUT value before substitution into commands (e.g., replace single quotes and wrap in single quotes).
2. Enforce anchored regex matching: automatically wrap validator regex in ^...$ if not already anchored, or use pcre2_match with PCRE2_ANCHORED.
3. Use argument-based execution instead of shell string interpolation: pass MANUALINPUT as an environment variable rather than substituting into the command string.
4. Add a character allowlist/blocklist for MANUALINPUT values to reject shell metacharacters ($, `, \, etc.).
